symptoms.symptom_name: Neurologically at baseline now — alert, oriented, no aura, no twitching or jerking

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: antiepileptic supply interruption, stable (urgent bridging, not emergency)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Being at baseline confirms the situation is urgent-but-stable, supporting an urgent same-day (T3) response rather than emergency escalation.
An alert patient with no motor activity and no confusion is not in status epilepticus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Overdue dose of a critical daily antiepileptic (sodium valproate 500 mg BD) with no supply on hand

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: antiepileptic supply interruption requiring urgent bridging
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An interrupted critical antiepileptic is the core problem and the driver of the time-critical bridging window.

symptoms.symptom_name: History of status epilepticus following a previously missed dose

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: antiepileptic supply interruption requiring urgent bridging
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Documented severe consequence of a prior missed dose sharply raises the stakes of tonight's overdue dose — this is why the bridging window is time-critical and must not be under-triaged.
Marks the patient as high-risk for status if a seizure begins — lowers the threshold to escalate on any seizure sign.

symptoms.symptom_name: No current jerking, loss of consciousness, confusion, or repeated seizures (screened negative)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative seizure screen excludes an active emergency and keeps the correct tier at urgent-same-day (T3) rather than 000.

symptoms.symptom_name: Neurological examination normal — GCS 15, cranial nerves grossly intact, normal global power and tone, no resting tremor or myoclonus

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured exam confirming baseline stability — sealed; the AI infers stability from the patient's self-report, not from an examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Vital signs within normal limits — BP 135/85, HR 98, Temp 36.7, RR 16, SpO2 100%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring but clinician-measured; the mild tachycardia is consistent with anxiety. Sealed — no home device offered these readings.

symptoms.symptom_name: High situational anxiety and psychomotor agitation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Explains the distress, but is a co-feature, not the whole picture — it must not eclipse the genuine medication-supply risk.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: antiepileptic supply interruption requiring urgent bridging
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anxiety neither confirms nor removes the pharmacological risk; the correct response addresses the supply, not just the distress.
